title: Multiply by 2 and 4
description: Multiply by 2 or 4 times a number less than or equal to 10.

hints:
We can think of $\blueD{2} \times \greenD{2}$ as $\blueD2$ rows of $\greenD2$ circles. How many circles are there?


[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large{\blueD{2} \times \greenD{2} = \purpleC{4}}$

We can think of $\blueD{2} \times \greenD{4}$ as $\blueD2$ rows of $\greenD4$ circles. How many circles are there?

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large\blueD2\\
\\
&\underline{\Large\times\Large\greenD4}\\
\\
&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large\purpleC8
\end{align}$

We can think of $\blueD{2} \times \greenD{5}$ as $\blueD2$ rows of $\greenD5$ circles. How many circles are there?


[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large{\blueD{2} \times \greenD{5} = \purpleC{10}}$

We can think of $\blueD{2} \times \greenD{7}$ as $\blueD2$ rows of $\greenD7$ circles. How many circles are there?


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large\blueD2\\
\\
&\underline{\Large\times\Large\greenD7}\\
\\
&~~\Large\purpleC{14}
\end{align}$

We can think of $\blueD{2} \times \greenD{9}$ as $\blueD2$ rows of $\greenD9$ circles. How many circles are there?


[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large{\blueD{2} \times \greenD{9} = \purpleC{18}}$

We can think of $\blueD{1} \times \greenD{2}$ as $\blueD1$ row of $\greenD2$ circles. How many circles are there?


[[☃ image 1]]

\\
&\underline{\Large\times\Large\greenD2}\\
\\
&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large\purpleC2
\end{align}$

[[☃ image 1]]

We can think of $\blueD{8} \times \greenD{2}$ as $\blueD8$ rows of $\greenD2$ circles. How many circles are there?

$\begin{align}&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large\blueD8\\
\\

\\
&~~\Large\purpleC{16}
\end{align}$

We can think of $\blueD{10} \times \greenD{2}$ as $\blueD{10}$ rows of $\greenD{2}$ circles. How many circles are there?


[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large{\blueD{10} \times \greenD{2} = \purpleC{20}}$
